titleStmt.title: Letter from Ellen Daly to Susan Daly, 25 March 1923
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Ellen Daly

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

correspAction.placeName: 'Our Lady of Mercy College, Carysfort, Blackrock, Dublin, Ireland '

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
P41/6/1/2/3 Saturday March 25 2 Dear Susie I am feeling very bad over your illness
if you were only said by me at Xmas this would
be the case now I trust in God, you will
be better when this reach you I had Kathleen
Durcan's letter I was glad to hear from her
K.M. + Molly are going to Dublin next
Thursday they will tell you all the
news. I thought you would be able
to come home but perhaps it is better
not as the journey would be too
much for you at present so to Whelan's
if you like you can call on all the
friends we are all well T.G. Your loving
Mother
P.S. Thank the Sisters that are so kind
to you for me I was speaking to Mrs. Mason
God & his B. Mother bring you home well.
M
